methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $103M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zeitview
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Oxa is a Series D autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, founded by Paul Newman and Ingmar Posner. The company has raised $103M to date. The relevant peer universe consists of five private companies operating in this sector (Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, Zeitview), with no public comparables identified.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. The Stage-Progression approach uses the $103M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $154M. A Peer-Median approach was not feasible: while four same-sector peers exist, insufficient datapoints met narrow filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier cohorts), and a broader all-sector median would introduce excessive noise and misalignment.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $131M to $178M, centered on $154M. The median estimate carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method and the absence of granular peer-stage and pricing data. Key risks include the nascent commercialization stage of autonomous systems broadly, which limits comparable transaction evidence, and sector-specific regulatory and technology adoption timelines that may not be reflected in historical stage-progression patterns. Investors should treat this range as indicative pending access to deeper peer transaction histories or market-rate Series D pricing in autonomous systems.
